senses.definition: a medical instrument used to measure the size of the prostate gland
examples: The doctor used a prostatometer to estimate the prostate’s size.
A prostatometer may help guide further tests.

senses.definition: surgery to remove part of the prostate and part of nearby muscle tissue
examples: The patient had a prostatomyomectomy to treat a growth.
Prostatomyomectomy is a specialized procedure.

senses.definition: an abnormal discharge of prostatic fluid from the prostate gland
examples: He was diagnosed with prostatorrhea after unusual fluid discharge.
Prostatorrhea can be noticed as repeated leakage.

senses.definition: an abnormal discharge of prostatic fluid from the prostate gland (British spelling variant of prostatorrhea)
examples: The report used the spelling prostatorrhoea.
Prostatorrhoea describes abnormal prostate fluid discharge.

senses.definition: a surgical cut into the prostate gland to treat a blockage or problem
examples: He underwent a prostatotomy to relieve urinary symptoms.
The surgeon explained what a prostatotomy involves.

senses.definition: relating to the prostate gland and the urinary bladder
examples: The imaging showed a prostatovesical connection.
The doctor discussed prostatovesical involvement.

senses.definition: surgery to remove part or all of the prostate and the seminal vesicles
examples: The patient required a prostatovesiculectomy for treatment.
Prostatovesiculectomy is performed in selected cases.

senses.definition: inflammation affecting the prostate gland and the seminal vesicles
examples: Prostatovesiculitis can cause pain and urinary discomfort.
He was treated for prostatovesiculitis after symptoms appeared.

senses.definition: a layer or structure in some organisms associated with the support of tissues (technical biology use)
examples: In the text, the prostemmate is described as a supportive structure.
Students reviewed prostemmate features under the microscope.

senses.definition: relating to the prostemmate (a technical term for a supportive or formative tissue structure in biology)
examples: The author used prostemmatic tissue to describe the region.
Prostemmatic cells are discussed in the diagram.

senses.definition: a front part of the sternum area in certain animals (technical anatomy use)
examples: The prostern is mentioned in the comparative anatomy section.
The diagram labels the prostern clearly.

senses.definition: front parts of the sternum area in certain animals (technical anatomy use)
examples: The prosternas are shown in the specimen’s illustration.
Researchers compared prosternas between species.

senses.definition: relating to the prostern or the front sternum area in certain animals (technical anatomy)
examples: The prosternal region was highlighted in the figure.
The prosternal plate is part of the animal’s structure.

senses.definition: having the form or position of a prostern (technical anatomy use)
examples: The prosternate structure is described as a front sternum form.
Students learned to recognize prosternate shapes in diagrams.

senses.definition: a front part of the sternum area in certain animals (technical anatomy)
examples: The prosternum is labeled in the anatomical diagram.
The prosternum helps describe the animal’s chest structure.

senses.definition: front parts of the sternum area in certain animals (plural of prosternum)
examples: The study compared prosternums across related species.
Different prosternums were described in the reference guide.

senses.definition: a prosthetic or appendage-like part in biology (technical term; exact meaning depends on the context)
examples: The text mentions a prostheca as a special body part.
Students reviewed how the prostheca functions in the organism.

senses.definition: strongening or supporting (technical use, often in biology or medical/chemical contexts)
examples: The prosthenic effect was mentioned in the article.
A prosthenic substance may be described as supportive.

senses.definition: artificial devices that replace a missing or damaged body part
examples: Modern prostheses can look and function more naturally.
She was fitted for new prostheses after surgery.

senses.definition: an artificial device that replaces a missing or damaged body part
examples: He uses a prosthesis for his leg.
The prosthesis was adjusted for comfort.

senses.definition: used to replace a missing body part, or designed to act like it
examples: She received a prosthetic arm.
The patient needs a prosthetic device for walking.

senses.definition: in a way that involves using a prosthetic device to replace or support a body part
examples: He can move more easily prosthetically after adjustment.
The surgeon rebuilt the area prosthetically.

senses.definition: the area of medicine that designs and builds prosthetic devices, and helps people use them
examples: He studies prosthetics at a training program.
She works in prosthetics and orthotics.

senses.definition: a specialist who designs and fits prosthetic devices for patients
examples: A prosthetist fitted his new leg prosthesis.
You’ll meet the prosthetist to adjust the device.

senses.definition: specialists who design and fit prosthetic devices for patients (plural of prosthetist)
examples: The clinic employs prosthetists for ongoing adjustments.
Prosthetists work with patients during rehabilitation.
